name: Turkmenistan

colors.symbolism: Islam, life, and the natural environment
the colour of the traditional carpet stripe — Turkmen carpets and the country's heritage of carpet weaving
the colour of the crescent and stars; peace and serenity
details of the carpet ornaments and the olive branches

description: A green field with a vertical red stripe along the hoist bearing five Turkmen carpet guls in vertical column, crossed olive branches at the bottom of the stripe, and in the upper fly a white crescent moon opening toward the fly with an arc of five white five-pointed stars beside it.
history: Adopted in its initial form on 19 February 1992, just months after Turkmenistan's independence. The carpet stripe with five guls was retained, but the design was modified on 1 February 1997 to add the olive branches commemorating Turkmenistan's UN-recognised permanent neutrality (1995); minor alterations to the proportions of the stripe and arrangement of the carpet guls were made on 24 January 2001. The current proportions (2:3) and exact ornament were fixed by presidential decree in 2008.

features.symbolism: a green field with a red vertical carpet stripe at the hoist bearing the traditional ornaments (guls) of the five Turkmen tribes; green for Islam and life, red for the heritage of Turkmen carpet-weaving
the five provinces (welayatlar) of Turkmenistan; the five-pointed shape of each star is said to symbolise the five states of matter in classical philosophy (solid, liquid, gas, crystal, plasma)

features.role: two crossed olive branches at the foot of the carpet stripe
features.symbolism: Turkmenistan's UN-recognised permanent neutrality, declared in 1995; the olive branches were added to the flag in 1997
